observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal General Services Officer to identify the discrepancies between the PPE
Ledger balances and RPCPPE to present the accurate balance of the PPE accounts in
accordance to Paragraph 27 of PPSAS 1. In addition, create a separate Inventory
Committee for one-time cleansing of PPE required under COA Circular No. 2020-006,
and approve the Physical Inventory Plan necessary for the conduct and complete
physical inventory.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the duly created
Inventory Committee, the General Services Office and Accounting Office to strictly
follow the general guidelines, procedural guidelines, reconciliation of inventory count
per RPCPPE with property and accounting records, disposition procedures for non-
existing/missing PPEs, procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/missing
PPEs without available records of accountability, applicable accounting entries and
required disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements required under COA
Circular No. 2020-006 in the conduct of inventory taking for one-time cleansing of
PPE.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor hire additional job order
personnel, if necessary, to be included in the members of the Inventory Committee or
replace the members of the committee who will be relieved of all their regular duties
to ensure that the Inventory Committee will devote their full time in the conduct of the
physical inventory taking until the same is completed and not to hamper the
operations of the Municipality during inventory taking. Lastly, we recommended that
the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide budgetary
requirements to cover the necessary expenses in the conduct of inventory taking.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and
General Services Officer to conduct complete reconciliation of Inventories as of
December 31, 2021 and submit to the Audit Team the corresponding adjusting
Journal Entry Voucher, if necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. General Services Officer to determine the existence of the Breeding Stocks
by conducting a comprehensive inventory of the Biological Assets
recorded in the books;

b. General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare the
required ledger and property stock cards, upon determination of the
assets’ existence; and

c. Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the General Services Officer to
reconcile the books of accounts with the results of physical inventory.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct Municipal Accountant and Bids
and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit the lacking documents noted above or
provide justification for further evaluation of the Audit Team, and require the BAC
abide with the posting requirements. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal
Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that future disbursement must be
supported with complete required documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: (a) the General Services Office (GSO) to take immediate action to facilitate the appraisal and disposal of the unserviceable properties as required by PD No. 1445 to prevent exposure to further deterioration of its values; (b) Committee to reconcile the result of the physical count of all PPEs with the property and accounting records; and (c) Municipal Accounting Office to perform necessary procedures to discover the nature and existence of the unidentified PPE.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require: GSO is still in the process of updating the actual status of all PPE included in the RPCPPE.
observations.reason: GSO is still in the process of facilitating disposal of unserviceable properties as pandemic and Taal Volcano crises hampered the execution of their duties and responsibilities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: a) the Inventory Committee, Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal GSO to reconcile the RPCPPE with the accounting and property records and identify the details of the discrepancies by working back on prior years’ transactions, and prepare the adjusting entries, if necessary; b) the Inventory Committee and Municipal GSO to ensure that the RPCPPE only includes properties that are physically identified and inventoried to establish the existence of each PPE; and c) Municipal Accounting Office and GSO to perform the necessary procedures to discover the nature and existence of the unidentified PPE.
observations.managementAction: GSO is still in the process of reconciling records with Accounting records.
observations.reason: GSO is still in the process of reconciling records with Accounting records. Short in manpower to facilitate and focus on inventory considering the pandemic situation and Taal Volcano crises that greatly hampered the execution of their duties and responsibilities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: a) Municipal Accountant and Municipal GSO - Designate to provide the complete description and cost segregation with corresponding cost components for other road projects which are still not accounted; b) Inventory Committee/Municipal GSO- Designate to conduct inventory count of all the Municipality’s road network systems and submit a Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System as of the end of the year and complete the necessary information in the Report of Local Road Network; c) Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the prescribed property ledger cards for Local Road Networks and disclose all required information in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted the Report of the Local Road Network.
observations.reason: Municipality submitted the Report of the Local Road Network. Engineering Office has yet to provide the complete description and cost segregation of road components for other projects which are still not accounted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: a) the Municipal Treasurer to review the accuracy of the List of Real Property Tax Collectibles provided at the beginning of the year and harmonize the same with the Quarterly Report on Real Property Assessment as of previous year-end or assessment roll generated by the Municipal Assessor’s Office; b) review and reconcile RPT and SET receivable balances; and c) ensure proper reconciliation and adjustments as necessary.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer reviewed the accuracy of the List of Real Property Tax Collectibles provided at the beginning of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Human Resources Management Office (HRMO) to immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents to conduct the proper review of payments of Terminal Leave Benefits, and ensure that future claim for said Terminal Leave Benefits is duly supported with the necessary documents required under COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO submitted the lacking documentary requirements. With regard to the “Affidavit of applicant that there is no pending criminal investigation or prosecution against him” of Former Mayor, the Municipality explained that there is no pending disallowance as of December 31, 2021. Nevertheless, the Municipality reiterated their request from Municipal Mayor to submit the affidavit regarding the payment of Terminal Leave Benefits.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Management stop procuring materials for repairs and maintenance thru cash advance and ensure that payments are made by check directly to the creditors to whom payments are due. Regular and recurring expenses should not be paid through reimbursement basis to comply with the stated provisions of law and ensure that the government treasury received the mandatory collections from business enterprises.
observations.managementAction: Management refrained from procuring materials for repair and maintenance thru cash advance. Likewise, ensure that payments are paid thru checks made directly to the creditor except during the emergency case wherein due to time constrains, they utilized reimbursement method on purchased related to ASF mitigation activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to enforce collection of the long outstanding receivables from the former Municipal Treasurer amounting to P778,239.36 by serving a written notice and demand letter, duly noted by the Municipal Mayor and received by Ms. Soledad S. Biscocho last September 28, 2020, demanding for settlement of the outstanding cash settlement of the obligation and stating the “threefold liability rule” which stated that although administrative and criminal liability may not be feasible, the Municipality can file and charge Ms. Biscocho for civil liability if she did not settled thereof.
observations.managementAction: Per written reply of Ms. Biscocho dated October 5, 2020, she noted by the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer sent letter, duly received by Ms. Soledad S. Biscocho last September 28, 2020, demanding for settlement of the outstanding cash settlement of the obligation stating the “threefold liability rule”.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer sent written notice and demand; permanent dismissal of her criminal case claiming civil liability was not proven by the prosecution.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents for further evaluation to the Audit Team; b. submit assessment of services indicating that the services of an event coordinator are not within their in-house capability; c. provide written pre-set qualifications/policies/guidelines for the hiring of event coordinator; d. provide reference documents indicating expected outputs/deliverables of the event coordinator; e. copy of Annual Procurement Plan showing the procurement for event coordinator; and f. accomplishment reports.
observations.managementAction: No action made.
observations.reason: Lacking documents yet submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a) MENRO - Designate to acquire a written contract between the Municipality and private sanitary landfill company covering the period for services from April to November 2019 in the amount of P163,429.00; and b) for further evaluation by the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: MENRO - Designate exerted all effort to acquire written contract, however the sanitary landfill was not cooperative for the matter. Starting October 1, 2021, they entered into new Memorandum of Agreement with Wastecon, Inc. for the tipping services.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: b) Enjoin the HRMO, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Treasurer for the proper and complete documentation before payment of wages of the job order personnel.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer coordinated to ensure the proper and complete documentation before payment of wages of the job order personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: a. adhere to the requirements of Paragraph C.3 Chapter 5 of the Manual on Property Custodianship and Section 266 of GAM for LGUs; b. conduct reconciliation of records for all PPE accounts and make necessary adjustments on the discrepancies between the accounting records and RPCPPE; c. submit the RPCPPE as required under Sec. 266 of GAM for LGUs; and d. regularly coordinate with the Municipal Assessor's Office in the conduct of physical count of land to determine the actual location of the lot.
observations.managementAction: GSO and Municipal Accountant adhere to the requirement of the aforementioned Manual. The Municipality submitted the RPCPPE as of December 31, 2021. The Municipal Assessor submitted a well prepared explanation regarding the discrepancy in the lot area covered by ARP No. 10-0015-00691. Likewise, the Municipality explained that 10 out of 17 lots are still untitled properties due to pending application with City Environmental and Natural Resources Office for Special Patent for Local Government Institutions. Lastly, the Municipality submitted copies of titles of 4 properties located at Brgy. Dayap-Itaas, Laurel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a) GSO to conduct a complete inventory of all inventory items as of December 31, 2021 and submit to the Audit Team to report and validate the non-existence of the inventories as of a certain date in accordance with the provisions of Sections 194 and 206 of the GAM for LGUs; b) GSO to adhere strictly with the prescribed submission of the RPCI once a year as provided under Section 194 of the GAM for LGUs; and c) Municipal Accountant and GSO to conduct regular reconciliation of their inventory records and reconcilia- tion balances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted RPCI as of December 31, 2021 to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Municipality submitted RPCI as of December 31, 2021 to the Audit Team. However, Accounting Office and GSO failed to conduct complete reconciliation of inventories as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC and BAC Secretariat to: a) Indicate in the RFQ the list of required documents based on the IRR of RA No. 9184 such as Mayor's Permit, Income/Business Tax Returns, Certificate of Platinum Membership, PhilGEPS Registration Number, Department of Trade and Industry Registration, BIR Certificate of Registration, the brand of the items and that the supplier can deliver to the Municipality, to be submitted by interest supplier at what stage of procurement it should be submitted; b) Ensure compliance on posting requirement of RFQ and Purchase Order under the alternative mode of procurement; c) Include in the BAC Resolution recommending for the award of the contract a categorical statement that the BAC validate the responsiveness of the supplier who submitted the Lowest Calculated quotation in the required documents; d) Revisit the rules and procedures of procurement particularly the updated IRR of RA No. 9184 as of March 31, 2021; and e) Attend any training and seminar relative to the Policy implementation of the procurement law in the local government.
observations.managementAction: The BAC revised the RFQ and required documents from supplier has been submitted and attached to the RFQ. The BAC complied on the posting requirement under alternative mode of procurement. The BAC revised the format of BAC Resolution to include the categorical statement to establish the accountability of the BAC that they have validate the responsiveness of the supplier that submitted the Lowest Calculated Quotation. The BAC revisited the rules and procedures of procurement and attended seminars. The Municipal Accountant reviewed and required the submission of compliance on posting requirement, BAC Resolution and Approved Procurement Plan before processing payment of procurement under the alternative method. The documents and pictures were submitted to the Audit Team last January 10, 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: a) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit the SAC Nos. and supporting SAC; b) Municipal Treasurer to make sure that payroll and its supporting documents are properly filled-out completely, properly signed and approved/certified by authorized officials; c) Municipal Treasurer to ensure that payrolls and other supporting documents are properly signed as required officials; d) Municipal Treasurer to make certain that all succeeding refunds are duly by Official Receipt as required by PD No. 1445; and e) Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to submit copy of liquidation report of P24,752,000.00 duly received by the DSWD.
observations.managementAction: Municipality submitted the SAC Nos. 3237, 2533, 505 and 491. Municipality ensured that all payroll and its supporting documents are properly filled-out, properly signed and certified by the authorized officials. Municipality ensured that refunds are now supported by Official Receipt. DSWD submitted copy of liquidation report duly received.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Accountant to submit lacking documentary requirements to support the payment of the purchased land as prescribed under PD No. 1445, COA Circular Nos. 2009-001, 2012-001 and 82-122-B and the IRR of RA No. 10752 for further evaluation of the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor submitted all lacking requirements last July 16, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a) DRRM Officer to distribute the donation in kind during the Taal Volcanic eruption to prepare and submit the Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of Donated Relief Goods, Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances, Acknowledgement Receipt, proof of receipt and distribution and inventory of remaining undistributed item, if any. b) Furnish the Audit Team with a one-time report submitted to the NDRRMC within 10 days after the end of each quarter. c) In addition, instruct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal General Services Officer and Municipal DRRM Officer to follow the required accounting and reporting guidelines as required in COA Circulars No. 2014-002 and 2020-009.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal DRRM Officer had submitted copies of LDRRMFIP and monthly Utilization Report to COA, Office of Civil Defense, DILG and DBM as required by the Joint Memorandum Circular.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Chairperson to: a) abide with the deadline set with other rules and regulation specified by PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 and other related rules and regulation; b) communicate in writing with the DILG regarding the status of the GPB for CY 2020; c) ensure the GPB is properly reviewed and endorsed by DILG before adoption; and d) adopt the online GAD Plan and Investment Plan Monitoring System where the Municipality needs to upload their GPB for proper review and monitoring.
observations.managementAction: GAD Chairperson abided with the deadline of submission of GAD Plan and Budget. Municipality submitted the status of the GPB for CY 2020. The Municipality adopted the online GAD Plan and Investment Plan Monitoring System where the Municipality uploaded their GPB.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all the Department Heads/employees for concerned to submit their detailed action plans on the findings and recommendations, including that of prior years and that the information gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation, and submit the same to the Audit Team. Moreover, follow strictly the above provisions of the law on the submission of the AAPSI within 60 days from the receipt of the COA Annual Audit report to give enough time for the Auditor to review, monitor and evaluate the action taken by the management on the audit recommendations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal AAPSI for CY 2020 submitted the AAPSI findings and recommendations including that of prior years.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:

a) the Inventory Committee, Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal General Services Office (GSO) to reconcile the RPCPPE with the accounting and property records and identify the details of the discrepancies by working back on prior yearstransactions, and prepare the adjusting entries, if necessary;

b) the Inventory Committee and Municipal GSO to ensure that the RPCPPE only includes properties that are physically identified and inventoried to establish the existence of each PPE; and

c) Municipal Accounting Office and GSO to perform the necessary procedures to discover the nature and existence of the unidentified PPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:

a) the Municipal Accountant to take necessary measures to settle the differences between the RPT Receivable/Deferred RPT and SET/Deferred SET;

b) the Municipal Treasurer to review the accuracy of the List of Real Property Tax Collectibles provided at the beginning of the year and harmonize the same with the Quarterly Report on Real Property Assessment as of previous year-end or assessment roll generated by the Municipal Assessors Office; and

c) the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the balance of RPT and SET Receivable as reported in the Municipals books of accounts and List of Delinquent Taxpayer as of December 31, 2019 and effect the necessary adjustments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting JEV to recognized the fund transfers as receivables from recipient barangay, unless the fund transferred has been liquidated by the concerned barangays, and ensure that financial transactions are properly recognized and recorded in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to:

a) refrain from utilizing the current years appropriation to pay prior years unrecorded obligation and to strictly adhere with existing laws, rules and regulations governing the expenditures within the limits of the current appropriations;

b) ensure that the funds are exclusively used for the purpose for which they were appropriated; and

c) see to it that all valid/lawful obligations are properly taken up in the accounts of the year they were incurred, pursuant to Section 350 of RA No. 7160 and Sections 4(1) and 119 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and HRMO to immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents to conduct the proper review of payments of Terminal Leave Benefits, and ensure that future claim for Terminal Leave Benefits is duly supported with the necessary documents required under COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Management to:

a) provide justification for the procurement of common-use supplies from private suppliers instead of from PS; and

b) ensure that future procurement of common-use supplies from private supplies will be supported by a Certificate of Non-availability of supplies in the PS.

We recommended that the Management stop the practice of procurement of materials for repairs and maintenance through cash advance and payment of claims through reimbursement. Likewise, ensure that payments are made by check directly to the creditors to whom payments are due. Regular and recurring expenses should not be paid through reimbursement basis to comply with the stated provisions of law and ensure that the government treasury received the mandatory collections from business enterprises.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all transfer of funds to Barangays be covered by a MOA and instruct the Municipal Accountant to require submission of other documentary requirements as enumerated in Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 before the release of financial assistance, cash or in kind, to establish proper accountability, and to ensure that the public funds were spent for the purposes they were requested and was expended economically, efficiently and effectively.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the recipient 8 Barangays to submit Liquidation Report with Summary of Check Issued and Summary of Cash Payment, certified by the Barangay Treasurer and approved by the Punong Barangay, as well as the necessary supporting documents used during the implementation of the projects.

Also, we recommended that the projects proposed to be funded out or to be charged from the Community Development Service fund be regularly monitored by the donor Municipality thru its monitoring team to ensure efficient and effective utilization of the funds and/or to prevent misuse or misapplication of the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to enforce collection of the long outstanding net receivables from the former Municipal Treasurer amounting to P778,239.36 by serving a written notice and demand settlement of the outstanding cash advances citing the provision of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012. Henceforth, strict compliance with existing rules and regulations regarding the settlement of cash advances and receivables should be applied.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

a) the concerned municipal officials to strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2012-002 in the utilization of LDRRMF;

b) require the Municipal Budget Officer to identify the correct charging of accounts whether for LCPC or LDRRMF; and

c) stop charging expenses to LDRRMF which may be charged to other funds of the Municipality to preserve the funds for disaster risk management activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a) Instigate a standard hiring process for engaging in job order contracts based on identified requirements/necessity of a particular office or department and the duties and responsibilities of the position to ensure that all job order contracts augments an existing need for manpower; and

b) Enjoin the HRMO, Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer for the proper and complete documentation before payment of wages of the job order personnel.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: a. the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all assets with useful life of more than one year but costing less than P10,000.00 are properly classified and recorded accordingly and to prepare journal entries and necessary adjustments on the PPE, Accumulated Depreciation and Government Equity accounts for a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements; b. the GSO to issue the corresponding Inventory ICS to all end-users of the small value tangible items and accordingly cancel the previously issued Acknowledge Receipt for Equipment (ARE); c. the GSO to take immediate action to facilitate the appraisal and disposal of the abovementioned unserviceable properties as required by PD No. 1445 to prevent exposure to different elements and further deterioration of its values; d. Committee to reconcile the result of the physical count of all PPEs with the property and accounting records.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Officer (GSO) started the issuance of the ICS to all end-users of the small value items procured after the issuance of the recommendation. Acknowledge- ment Receipt for Equipment (ARE) issued; The GSO started the issuance of the ICS to all end-users of the small value items procured after the issuance of the recommendation. Acknowledge- ment Receipt for Equipment (ARE) issued; The GSO to facilitate the still failed to facilitate the appraisal and disposal of the above-mentioned unserviceable properties. No Management action yet. Committee still failed to reconcile the result of the physical count of all PPEs with the property and accounting records.
observations.reason: Journal entries to affect the recorded balances of the account with the GSO records specifically recorded inclusion of small value items and not all items. were not yet recorded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Management to: a. regularly reconcile the balances in the accounting records with the GSIS in order to ascertain the reliability of the amount Due to GSIS in the financial statements; and b. effect timely and accurate remittance of GSIS premiums, loans and contributions.
observations.managementAction: The Management had already remitted some of the unpaid premiums to GSIS. Management has advised the concerned employees to settle their outstanding loans to the GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to: a. ensure that the LDRRMFIP contains the details of the projects and activities to be funded in the LDRRMFIP as required under Section 6.2 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-1 and Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; b. meticulously plan the specific programs and activities to be implemented in the prevention, mitigation and preparedness of the Municipality in case of calamity and ensure the immediate implementation; and c. coordinate with other Municipal Offices such as the Bids and Awards Committee and Engineering Office for the proper and timely implementation of scheduled projects, programs and activities.
observations.managementAction: The Management has complied with the presentation of details of its programs, projects and activities to be funded in the LDRRMFIP. The Management had coordinated with Municipal offices involved in the implementation of scheduled projects, programs and activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point Person to: a. establish a Technical GAD Database and sex-disaggregated data as required under Section 4(b) of the Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 of the DILG, DBM, NEDA and PCW; b. direct the GAD Focal Point Person to monitor and report regularly the status of implementation of the GAD Plan in order to address the gender issues; and c. utilize the fund solely for GAD related activities or to programs that are gender-responsive and are aligned to the GAD mandate.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person informed the Audit Team that they started to establish the GAD database and sex-disaggregated data in the year 2019, however, it was stopped due to the National Elections and promised to adhere to the Audit Team's recommendation. Programs included in GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) for CY 2019 were implemented by the Municipality. Certificate of Review and Endorsement issued by the DILG-Batangas Director regarding the Municipality's GPB for CY 2019 states that 'All Programs, Projects and Activities (PPAs) are responsive to LGUs identified gender issue and/or GAD mandate.'

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require:
a. the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all small value assets with useful life of
more than one year but costing less than P10,000.00 are properly classified and
recorded accordingly and to prepare journal entries and effect the necessary
adjustments on the PPE, Accumulated Depreciation and Government Equity
accounts for a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements;
b. the General Services Officer (GSO) to issue the corresponding Inventory
Custodian Slip (ICS) to all the end-users of the small tangible items and to
cancel the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) previously issued;
c. the GSO to take immediate action to facilitate the appraisal and disposal of the
abovementioned unserviceable properties as required by Presidential Decree
(PD) No. 1445 to prevent exposure to different elements and further
deterioration of its values;
d. a Committee to reconcile the result of the physical count of all PPEs with the
property and accounting records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant in
coordination with the Municipal Human Resource and Management (HRM) Office
to:
a. regularly reconcile the balances in accounting records with the records of the
GSIS in order to ascertain the reliability of the amount Due to GSIS in the
financial statements; and
b. effect timely and accurate remittance of GSIS premiums, loans and
contributions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to:
a. ensure that the allocation for disaster, mitigation, prevention and preparedness
as resented in the LDRRMFIP contains the details of the projects and activities
to be funded, including those planned for the utilization of the previous year’s
LDRRMF as required under Section 6.2 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint
Circular No. 2013-1 and Items 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002;
b. meticulously plan the specific programs and projects necessary in the
prevention, mitigation and preparedness of the Municipality in case of calamity
and ensure the immediate implementation; and
c. coordinate with other concerned Municipal Offices such as the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) and Municipal Engineering Office for the proper and
timely implementation of scheduled projects, programs and activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point Person to:
a. establish a GAD Database and sex-disaggregated data as required under
Section 4(b) of JMC No. 2013-01 of the DILG, DBM, NEDA and the PCW in
order to create a well-defined programs and projects that are best suited to the
need of its constituents;
b. direct the GAD Focal Point Person to properly monitor and report regularly the
status of implementation of the activities stated in the Gender and Development
Plan in order to address the gender issues; and
c. utilize the fund solely for GAD related activities or to specific programs that are
gender-responsive and are aligned to the GAD mandate.

observations.managementAction:
The GSO started reclassifying all items and made a new PPE.
The concerned personnel in the GSO reconcile the result of the physical count of all PPE with the property and accounting records and submit in the prescribed form the RPCPPE and submit to the Auditor not later than January 31, of each year.
The concerned personnel coordinated records with the Office of the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of the records.
observations.reason:
The GSO is still in the process of reclassifying each item in the RCPPE.
The GSO is still in the process of reconciliation of coordinated records with the Office of the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of the records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
• revert those payables found not supported by any valid document to support the legality and validity of the claim; 
• seek authority from Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion of the total amount of P30,317.21 to Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund; and
• refrain from recording payables without complete and valid supporting documents to ensure fair presentation of the payables account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
The Accountant reverted those payables found not supported by any valid document to amounting to P30,317.21 to Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund.
The Municipal Accountant requested the Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion of the total amount of P30,317.21 to Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund.
The Municipal Accountant refrained from recording payables without complete and valid supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point Person to:
• establish a GAD Database and sex-disaggregated data as required under Section 4(b) of JMC No. 2013-01 of the DILG, DBM, NEDA and the PCW in order to create a well-defined programs and projects that are best suited to the need of its constituents; 
• direct the GAD Focal Point Person to properly monitor and report regularly the status of implementation of the activities stated in the Gender and Development Plan in order to address the gender issues; and
• utilize the fund solely for GAD related activities or to specific programs that are gender-responsive and are aligned to the GAD mandate.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality used the CBMS 2015 as basis of GAD Plan and Budget in the absence of GAD data base.
GAD Technical Working Group was conducted last October 3-4, 2017 to identify gender issues, plan and monitor the implementation of GAD Plan.
Members of GPFS-TWG attended the Gender Sensitivity Training last November 27-29, 2018 at Capitol, Batangas to familiarize and enhance their capability when it comes to GAD Planning and Budgeting Formulation.
The Management included a GAD database activity in the GAD Plan and Budget 2019 for better identification of gender issues.
observations.reason: The Municipality has yet to establish a GAD data base.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the department head/s or personnel to fast tract the submission of the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) of the CY 2016 Annual Audit Report to the Auditor concerned to facilitate monitoring and validations of the related audit observations and recommendations, and eventually to submit a validation report to the Regional Office IV-A, for monitoring purposes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted the AAPSI to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal accounting and financial reports to be strictly submitted on time within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction:
The concerned officers to look into the root cause of the delay in the timely preparation and submission of the accounting and financial reports and effect appropriate action/s when necessary, to make possible the early submission of the accounting and financial reports.
The Management assured the immediate submission of the required accounting and financial reports within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• ensure that a list of all on-going government PPAs and all infrastructure and non-infrastructure projects respectively, and those to be implemented during the year including relevant information on the programs/activities for implementation by the concerned department be submitted to the Supervising Auditor/Audit Team at the beginning of the year; and
• enjoin all concerned Department Heads, through the posting to the concerned offices of the signboards of the programs/activities for implementation, will post the signboards of the programs/activities for implementation as required of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction:
The GSO submitted the list of all on-going government PPAs in all infrastructure projects, projects and those to be implemented during the year including relevant information for implementation.
The concerned Department Heads, through the coordination of the concerned Heads, will post the signboards of the programs/activities for implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) to prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (MDRRM) reports/documents and submit all the required reports/documents to the concerned agencies within the prescribed period in consonance with the afore-cited guidelines/issuances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted the required Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to the concerned agencies and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the GSO to:
• conduct detailed review of the documents maintained to
  determine the correct classification of each PPE for the
  consequent classification and adjustments to ensure the
  accuracy of the inventory and accounting records; and
• direct the personnel concerned to reconcile the result of the
  physical count of all PPE with the property and accounting
  records and submit in the prescribed form the RPCPPE and
  submit to the Auditor not later than January 31, of each year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to:
• revert those payables found not supported by any valid document to
  support the legality and validity of the claim;
• seek authority from the Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion of the
  total amount of P30,317.21 to Unappropriated Surplus of the General
  Fund; and
• refrain from recording payables without valid documents to support
  the legality and validity of the claim for the fair presentation of the
  payables account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Pint
Person to:
• establish a GAD Database and Sex-disaggregated data as required
  under Section 4(b) of JMC No. 2013-01 of the DILG, DBM, NEDA
  and the PCW in order to create a well-defined programs and projects
  that are best suited to the need of its constituents;
• direct the GAD Focal Point Person to properly monitor and report
  regularly the status of implementation of the activities stated in the
  Gender and Development Plan in order to address the gender issues;
  and
• utilize the fund solely for GAD related activities or to specific
  programs that are gender-responsive and are aligned to the GAD
  mandate.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the assigned
department head/s or personnel to fast tract the submission of the Agency Action
Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) of the CY 2016 Annual Audit
Report to the Auditor concerned to facilitate monitoring and validations of the
actions taken by the LGU on the related audit observations and
recommendations, and eventually to submit a validation report to the Regional
Office IV-A, for monitoring purposes. Compliance of the same report to the
above mentioned oversight bodies is necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to strictly enforce the submission of required accounting and
financial reports within the prescribed period.

The concerned officers to look into the root cause of the delay in the
preparation and submission of the financial reports and effect appropriate
action/s when necessary, to make possible the early submission of the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• direct the Municipal Engineer and the General Services Officer to
  ensure that a list of all on-going government PPAs in all
  infrastructure and non-infrastructure projects, respectively, and those
  to be implemented during the year including relevant information
  thereof is submitted to the Supervising Auditor/Audit Team Leader at
  the beginning of the year; and
• enjoin all Department Heads concerned of the posting to appropriate
  signboards of the programs/activities for implementation by the
  concerned department as required of COA Circular No. 2013-004.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the certified Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization
of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund and the Municipal Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management Officer to submit all the required
reports/documents to the concerned agencies within the prescribed period in
consonance with the afore-cited guidelines /issuances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: a. The GSO and the Inventory Committee to conduct inventory of all the municipal property, including local road networks and submit the Inventory report thereon to the Audit Team; and b. The Municipal Accountant to maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards to support the recorded PPE in the books and to facilitate reconciliation of the balances of the account with the GSO records.
observations.managementAction: The GSO Inventory had already conducted the consolidated the municipal inventory of all property, including local road networks and in the process of consolidation of the Local Road Network System with the Engineering Office. The Municipal Accountant to maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards to support the recorded PPE in the books and to facilitate reconciliation of the balances of the account with the GSO records.
observations.reason: The GSO have not yet consolidated the Local Road Network System. Not Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 1.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to enforce collection from the cooperative and remit the collection immediately to the Office of the President. First instalment of P50,000.00 every October of the year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality received the full amount.
observations.reason: They have not yet collected the payment amounting to P50,000.00 last October 24, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to remit the unexpended balances of the national funds due to GOCCs and LGUs totalling P19,105.12 to the General Fund treating them as income of the Municipality; and determine the purpose of the financial assistance granted by DSWD in 1995 and thereafter the Municipality may utilize the amount for the same purpose it was granted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had remitted the unexpended balances of the national funds to Bureau of Treasury and transfer the balances Due to GOCCs and LGUs to the General Fund and recorded it as income.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: • All the officials and employees of the municipality to liquidate cash advances granted through the submission of complete documents such as bus tickets, certificate of appearance and other documents immediately or within 30 days after return to official station and refund the excess, if any. • The Municipal Accountant to ensure that liquidation vouchers were supported with complete documents to avoid suspension of the cash advance granted and refrain from processing new cash advance if the previous cash advance is not yet liquidated. • The Municipal Accountant to immediately issue demand letter to employees and officials who failed to liquidate cash advances within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: All the officials and employees were directed to liquidate cash advances on time with the submission of all the documents. The Municipal management assured that no employees had been granted another cash advance if previous cash advance is not yet liquidated. The Municipal Accountant to ensure that liquidation vouchers were supported with complete documents to avoid suspension of the cash advance granted and refrain from processing new cash advance if the previous cash advance is not yet liquidated. The Municipal Accountant to immediately issue demand letter to employees and officials who failed to liquidate cash advances within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to strictly observe the various provisions of RA No. 7160 and NBC No. 563 in the appropriation and payment of membership fees and annual dues to various leagues and ensure that payments of such kind to private organization shall not be charged against the government fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Management has no more appropriation and payment for membership fees and annual dues to various leagues for the year 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GSO personnel to make full inventory of all the vehicles that are not yet insured and cause the processing of their insurance from the GSIS. Likewise, provide adequate insurance coverage for all the government buildings in the General and SEF and guarantee indemnification in case of occurrence of catastrophic perils.
observations.managementAction: The GSO has made full inventory of all the vehicles and the processing of insurance from the GSIS. Likewise, provide adequate insurance coverage and the management will process the insurance for the building on 1st quarter of 2018.
observations.reason: The building was not included in the Annual Investment Plan for the year 2017. The management will process the insurance for the building on 1st quarter of 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to ensure that the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness as presented in the LDRRMFIP are utilized in full to avoid or prevent the adverse impacts in case a disaster or calamity occurs in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMC passed a resolution last September 4, 2017 for the realignment, release and utilization of the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF Fund to Sangguniang Bayan and approved thru SB Resolution No. 102, Series of 2017 on October 9, 2017 Realign the purchase of Office Equipment, Furniture and Fixtures to Disaster or Rescue Equipment. Implementation of the Projects/Activities. Most of the projects have been completed and on-going on last quarter of 2017.
observations.reason: Not yet realigned the purchase of Equipment, Furniture and Fixtures to Disaster or Rescue Equipment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point Person to properly monitor and report regularly the status of implementation of the activities stated in the GAD Plan in order to address the gender issues. Ensure that the programs and projects included in the GAD Plan are gender responsive. Likewise, utilize the fund solely for GAD related activities or to specific programs that are gender-responsive and are aligned to the GAD mandate.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal had provided necessary documents to expedite the implementation under BUB-UNDP-DSWD. United Nation Development Program (UNDP) has to use alternative mode of procurement due to failed bidding. The Municipal will utilize the fund solely for GAD related activities or to specific programs that are gender-responsive and are aligned to the GAD mandate.
observations.reason: The Management has not yet liquidated the amount intended for the project under BUB-UNDP-DSWD.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • Require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat and the BAC Technical Working Group (TWG) to submit all the lacking documents therein to support the propriety of the procurement made by the Municipal Government; • Direct the Municipal Health Officer (MHO) to ensure that all the drugs and medicines to be procured are accompanied with certification that the drug products being requisitioned or procured fall within and conform with the PNDF Vol. I, current edition and included in the Essential Drugs List; otherwise, a written request with justification should be submitted to the Head of the national policy Staff for approval of the purchase to avoid disallowance in audit; and • Direct the BAC to verify and validate all the documents submitted by the suppliers/contractors before declaring a winner bidder.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat and BAC has already submitted the lacking documents therein to support the propriety of the procurement made by the Municipal Government; The MHO ensures that succeeding purchases of medicines were included in the Essential Drug List of PNDF Vol. 1, current edition. The BAC verifies and validates all the documents of the supplier/contractors before declaring the winning bidder.
observations.reason: All succeeding purchases of medicines were included in the Essential Drug List of PNDF Vol. 1, current edition.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:

the Municipal Treasurer to issue demand letter for the collection of the outstanding balance of LAMOEMCO and remit the amount to the Office of          the       President, Presidential Management Staff. 

 the Municipal  Accountant to record the collection and remittance in the books. Also, see to it that fund transfers are properly accounted for.

the concerned office to see to it that fund transfers are properly accounted for and the implementation is closely monitored and supervised to achieve efficiency and effectiveness in operations.
observations.managementAction:
It was agreed upon during the General Assembly held on March 18, 2017 that the cooperative will pay the amount of P50,000.00 to the Municipality and the Municipality will  remit the amount to the OP-PMS.   


Fund transfers are duly recorded. No collection was made during the year.  




Fund transfer if any, is duly recorded and implementation is properly monitored.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:

the GSO  and the Inventory Committee to conduct complete inventory of all the Municipal   property including local road networks system and submit  the Inventory Report  thereon to the Audit Team; and
the Municipal Accountant   to maintain complete and updated  PPE Ledger Cards  to  support the recorded PPE in the books and to facilitate reconciliation of the balances of the account with the GSO records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to enforce collection of P50,000.00 every October of the  year from  the cooperative  and remit the collection immediately to the Office of the President.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to remit to the Bureau of Treasury the unexpended balances of the national funds and transfer the balances Due to GOCCs and LGUs totaling P19,105.12 to the General Fund treating them as income of the Municipality; and determine the  purpose of the financial assistance granted by the DSWD in 1995 and thereafter the Municipality may utilize the amount for the  same purpose it was granted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
all the officials and employees of the municipality to liquidate all the cash advances granted through the submission of all the documents such as  bus tickets, certificate of appearance and other documents immediately or within 30 days after return to official station and refund the excess, if any;
the Municipal Accountant to ensure that liquidation vouchers were supported with complete documents to avoid suspension of the cash advance granted and refrain from processing new cash advance if previous cash advance is not yet liquidated.
the Municipal Accountant to immediately issue demand letter to employees and officials who failed to liquidate cash advances within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to strictly observe the provisions of RA No. 7160 and NBC No. 563 in the appropriation and payment of membership fees and annual dues to various leagues and ensure that payments of such kind to private organization shall not be charged against the government funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GSO personnel to make full inventory of all the vehicles which are  not yet insured and cause the processing of their insurance from the GSIS. Likewise, provide adequate insurance coverage for all the government buildings in the General and SEF to guarantee indemnification in case of occurrence of catastrophic perils.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to ensure that the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness as presented in the LDRRMFIP are utilized in full to avoid or prevent the adverse impacts in case a disaster or calamity occurs in the Municipality.  Realign the purchase of Office Equipment, Furniture and Fixtures to Disaster or Rescue Equipment for proper utilization thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point Person to properly monitor and report regularly the status of implementation of the activities stated in the GAD Plan in order to address the gender issues. Ensure that the programs and projects included in the GAD Plan are gender responsive processes, procedures are properly followed and other factors that would affect the implementation must be considered to avoid problems when it comes to implementation. Likewise, utilize the fund solely for GAD related activities or to specific programs that are gender-responsive and are aligned to the GAD mandate.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat and the BAC Technical Working Group (TWG) to submit all the lacking documents noted therein to support the propriety of the procurement made by the Municipal  Government;
direct the Municipal Health Officer to  ensure that all the  drugs and medicines to be procured are accompanied with certification that the  drug products being requisitioned or procured fall within and conform with PNDF Vol. I, current edition and included in the  Essential Drugs  List of the PNDF otherwise, a written request  with justification should  be submitted to the Head  of the National Policy Staff for approval of the purchase to avoid disallowance in audit; and
direct the BAC  to verify and validate all the documents submitted by the suppliers/contractors before declaring a winning bidder.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:

the General Services Officer to  conduct  complete inventory of all the Municipal properties and  submit  the Inventory Report thereon. 

the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer  maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, to facilitate the conduct of physical inventory and reconciliation of balances of the account.
observations.managementAction:
The GSO was not able to conduct inventory in 2016, however, they already started inventory taking in March 2017. 


The GSO already maintains Property Cards but the Accounting Office only started to prepare the PPE ledger cards in 2017.
observations.reason:
Lack of manpower in the GSO, change of administration and change of GSO-designate.

Reiterated in  Part II, Observation   No. 1

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers with corresponding amount of taxes due and collectible for the year as basis for the recording of actual RPT and SET receivables at the beginning of the year.  Reconcile year-end balances of RPT and SET receivables with the Schedule of Delinquent accounts to establish the correct amount of the receivable accounts presented in the financial statements as of December 31, 2015.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer submitted to the Municipal Accountant a certification of the total amount due and collectible based on the records of the list of taxpayers and amount due and collectible maintained by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended  that the Municipal Mayor direct the GSO and others concerned to      conduct   a    complete physical inventory taking of all the supplies of the Municipality;  prepare Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) of all offices every semester and submit copies to the Auditor and Municipal Accountant; and reconcile  the inventory report  with the subsidiary/general ledger  balances to establish the correct balances as presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The GSO submitted a Report of Inventory of all the supplies as of December 31, 2016   to the Audit Team and reconciled the same with accounting records.

Community Based Monitoring System has been formulated by the end of CY 2015 which served as database of each program implemented in the LGU specially  GAD Plan and Budget Programs. 




GAD Training/Capability Building was conducted and attended by  50 men/women participants (GFPS-TWG) and other employees for them be aware of  the formulation of GAD Plan and Budget.  

The GAD Focal Point system is already in place and functioning as required, thru the headship of the GAD Focal Point Person. 





The Focal Point Person prepares all the  reporting requirements as required in the JMC No. 2013-001 and monitor compliance  and implementation of the GAD plans/programs and activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a check amounting to P100,000.00 payable to the Bureau of Treasury to remit the PDAF balance in compliance with Supreme Court Decision G.R. No. 208566.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has already deposited the amount of P100,000.00  to the Bureau of Treasury on January 30, 2017 under Check No. 555655
